name: SAST-Backdoor-PoCs
summary:
SAST Scan Blindspot Backdoors
Example of vulnerable code with supporting exploit implementation whose data/control flow make it difficult for eneterprise static vulnerability scanners to detect problems with.

documentation.readme:
SAST Scan Blindspot Backdoors
Example of vulnerable code with supporting exploit implementation whose data/control flow make it difficult for eneterprise static vulnerability scanners to detect problems with.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with brettsm/vsftpd2.3.4-backdoor-exploit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with boudarbiss/RAT-STA (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bitgodhack/AndroidHack_BackDoor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='post_exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'post_exploit' phase against a target with '['web', 'shell_windows', 'shell_linux']' as the attack surface (command_and_control, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Command and Control system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
